english: February which is different has just twenty-eight days, until leap year; and then it has *twenty-nine* days.
He/she (turned) four on *the 26th* of this month.
[Beginning Cherokee]
I shot a squirrel with a. 22 rifle.
Let's you and I have coffee.
Do you take (mix) *cream* in it?
My grandmother bakes really *delicious* cornbread!
You can start immediately
The *boy* is chasing the dogs.
… sometimes *praying*.
leave there *thy gift* before the altar, and go …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎯ *ᏣᏓᏅᏖᏗ* ᎪᎦ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ …
english: … And for this *thou must consider* to whom he sent thee …

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᎪᎲᎩ ᎥᏓᎸ *ᎠᏓᎨᏗ* ᏗᎬᏩᎸᏌᏛ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎤᏓᏑᏱ ᎨᏒᎩ …
english: and I saw as a sea of *glass* mingled with fire …
The fearless man *jumped*.
and the first *house* you come at is his.

syllabary: … ᎯᏄᎪᎢ ᏣᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏣᎪᏏ ᎨᏒ ᎩᏯᏓᏅᏏ, *ᎠᎴ ᏣᏙᏓ ᎦᏁᎸ ᎭᏓᏅᎾ* …
english: … 'Go for thyself, from thy land, and from thy kindred, and *from the house of thy father* …
Fifth: The dough was collected into *the stove*.

syllabary: … ᏧᏁᎶᏗ *ᎤᏓᏛᏛᏅᎩ* ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᎨᏍᎩᏰᏅᏕᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎦᎨᏛ ᏥᎦᎵᏎᎭ ᎤᏛᏅᎩ …
english: … Christian *asked him* further If he could not help him off with his Burden that was upon his back …
An elephant *is bathing* in a lake.
and lo, *a leper* having come, was bowing to him …

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ *ᎠᎾᏕᎶᏆᏍᎪᎢ*, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏱᏩᏂᏰᎢᎶᎯᎰ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
english: always *learning*, and never to a knowledge of truth able to come,
at the sight also of whom he began to blush *for shame*.
… ‘In sins thou wast *born* altogether …

syllabary: … ᎥᏝ ᎠᏏ ᎦᏙ ᏱᎬᏩᏍᏗ ᎤᏟ ᎤᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ *ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗ* ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏱᎦᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏂ ᏨᏗᏣᏎᎮᎸ …
english: … there is not a more dangerous and *troublesome* way in the world than is that unto which he hath directed thee …

syllabary: … ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, Ꭷ, ᎰᏩᏉ, *ᎦᏕᎶᎣᏍᎦᏍᎩᏂ* ᎠᏂ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏣᏆᏒᎦᏔ.
english: … He answered, Sir, I *perceive* by the Book in my hand, that I am condemned to die.
Truly, *said* Christian, I do not know.
and she saith, '*Drink*, my lord;' …
“*You should be learning* doctoring medicine …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎭᏙᎴᎰᎯ, ᎦᎪ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ ᎯᎠ, ᎠᎵᏰᏑᏍᏚᏬ ᎠᏐᎥᏍᏙᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎸᏌᏛᏗ, ᎠᎴ *ᎠᏙᎳᏅᏍᏗ*.
english: … and she saith, 'Discern, I pray thee, whose [are] these -- the seal, and the ribbons, and the *staff*.'
… the sin *revived*, and I died;

syllabary: … ᏗᏇᏅᏒ ᏔᏘᏄᎦ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ, ᎠᎴ ᏘᎷᎦ ᎠᎴ *ᎭᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓ* …
english: … 'Bring the men into the house, and slaughter an animal, and *make ready* …
… and then when *it is shined on by the sun* the water is dried by the sun.

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏂᎥᎩ, ᎠᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎤᎶᏏᎶᏛ ᎠᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ, ᎠᎴ *ᎠᎦᏴᎵ* ᎤᎦᏴᎳᏥᎶᏛ.
english: — and the number of them was myriads of myriads, and *thousands* of thousands —
… and both were *advanced in their days*.
Actually, *a woman* was being killed.

syllabary: ᏥᏌ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ *ᎤᎩᏒᎩ* ᎦᏚ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏁᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏣᏗ ᏚᏅᏁᎸᎩ.
english: Jesus, therefore, doth come and *take* the bread and give to them, and the fish in like manner;

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏓᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᏡᎦ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎥᏝ *ᏣᎩᏍᏗ* ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᎯᏳᏰᏃ ᎢᎦ ᎯᎬᎭ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏒ ᏣᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
english: and of the tree of knowledge of good and evil, thou dost not *eat* of it, for in the day of thine eating of it -- dying thou dost die.'
*receive*, I pray thee, my blessing …

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ *ᏥᎩᎵᏲᎦ*; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎦᏕᎣᏍᎦ; …
english: for which cause also these things *I suffer*, but I am not ashamed, …
… and all the green grass *was burnt up*.

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎩ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᏚᎦᏒᏍᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎠᎪᎲᏍᎬᎢ* …
english: and were crying, seeing the smoke of *her burning* …
… *and as he read*, he wept and trembled;
for already certain *did turn aside* after the Adversary.

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳ ᎩᎶ *ᎠᎪᎾ* ᎤᏲᏏᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᏧᏪᏅᏒ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ; …
english: and if any one is *hungry*, at home let him eat, …
Yes, *I see it* well.
… for often he doth fall into the fire, and *often into the water*,
‘But thou, fasting, anoint thy head, and *wash thy face*,
*First* they plant seed.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ *ᎠᏓ* ᎣᏍᏛ ᏚᏝᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎸᎴ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎡᏏᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎨᎳᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏢᏁᎢ, *ᎠᏓ* ᏕᎠᏢ ᎦᏚᎢ.
english: … and arrangeth *the wood*, and bindeth Isaac his son, and placeth him upon the altar above *the wood*;

syllabary: ᎠᏥᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏗᎠᏥᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏈᏓ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, *ᎯᎷᎦ* ᎠᎴ ᎭᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎪᏎᎴᎢ.
english: and there came a voice unto him: ‘Having risen, Peter, *slay* and eat.’
‘On the succeeding day, also, he shewed himself to them as *they are striving* …

syllabary: ᏧᏁᎶᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᎯᏍᏛ *ᎠᎾᎵᏃᎮᏔᏂᏐ* ᎠᎾᎢᏒ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒ.
english: Christian and Pliable *went talking* over the Plain; and thus they began their discourse.

phonetic:
[ᎹᎦ 6:2]
Literally - “And they often have holiday - *it did come to that point*.”
english: “And the sabbath *having come*.”
… and because *he had bound him*,

syllabary: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏱᏁᎬᏩᏍᏗᏉ ᎾᎿ *ᎤᏁᏍᎨᎲᎢ*, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎦᎫᏴᎡᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
english: if of any one the work doth remain that *he built* on [it], a wage he shall receive;

syllabary: … ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏅᏅᎩ ᏛᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏂᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏛᎾᏗᏔᎯᏒᎩ, ᏚᎾᎴᏅᎩ *ᎤᎾᏁᏦᏅᎩ*.
english: … ‘The people sat down to eat and to drink, and stood up to *play*;’

syllabary: ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ *ᎠᎰᎵ* ᏭᏴᎸᎯ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏱᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ, *ᎠᎰᎵᏍᎩᏂ* ᏅᏓᏳᏄᎪᏨᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏄᏩᏁᎰ ᏴᏫ.
english: not that which is coming into *the mouth* doth defile the man, but that which is coming forth from *the mouth*, this defileth the man.’

syllabary: ᎾᏉᏃ ᏮᎤᏨᏒᎩ, ᎠᏴᏃ ᎡᏍᎦ ᏛᎩᎶᏒᎩ; ᏄᏬᎯᏳᎯᏒᎾ *ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ*, ᎠᏴᏃ ᎠᏂ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᏙᏛᏊᎪᏔᏅᎩ.
english: so he went his way, and I came mine: *he after* Obstinate, and I to this Gate.
*He is eating* corn and beans.

syllabary: ᎢᏳᏃ ᏦᏰᏂ ᎯᎦᏘᏏ ᏕᏦᏕᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, *ᎯᏍᏆᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ*, ᎠᎴ ᏣᏕᎨᏍᏗ …
english: ‘And, if thy right hand doth cause thee to stumble, *cut it off*, and cast from thee …
I do not *ask* that Thou mayest take them out of the world …

syllabary: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏤᎲ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᎬᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ *ᎠᏔᏲᏎᎮᏍᏗ*, …
english: and if any of you do lack wisdom, *let him ask from* God, …

syllabary: ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏧᎾᎩᎸᏗ *ᎤᎾᏘᏅᏍᏗ* ᏄᏅᏁᎸ, ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏨᏒᎩ, ᏗᏐᏴ ᏭᏂᎶᏒᎩ.
english: and on the morrow, having suffered the horsemen *to go on with* him, they returned to the castle;

syllabary: … ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᎦᏓᏅᏛ ᎬᏯᏂᎩᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᏗᎧᏃᎩᏛ ᎬᏗ, ᎠᎴ *ᎠᎱᎵ* ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎬᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏏᎳᏕᏫᏛ ᏗᎧᏃᎩᏍᏙᏗ ᎬᏗ?
english: … I send thee away with joy and with songs, with *tabret* and with harp,

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎢᏨᏲᏎᎭ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᏛᎿᏕᎩ ᎤᏬᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ *ᎠᎲᎦ* ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏐᎢ.
english: and he said, ‘Truly I say to you, that this poor widow *did cast in* more than all;

syllabary: … ᎿᏉ ᏦᎢ ᎾᏕᏘᏴ ᏥᎷᎬᎩ ᎠᎩᏲᎲᎩ ᎤᏓᏔᏅᎯ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎦᏔᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏡᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ *ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᏩᏘᎭ*; ᎯᎴᏴᏍᏓ; ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᎤᎨᏗ ᎦᏙᎯ?
english: … three years I come seeking fruit in this fig-tree, and *do not find*, cut it off, why also the ground doth it render useless?

syllabary: ᎡᏥ ᏲᎾ ᎤᏪᏅᏎ *ᎠᏫ* ᏤᏙᎲᎢ. “ᎯᎪᏩᏘᎭᏍ ᎠᎩᎧᎲ ᏲᎾ ᎠᏓ?”
english: Mother bear went to where the *deer* walk about. “Have you seen my baby bear?”
He *who is sowing* *doth sow* the word;
… all *thy body* shall be enlightened,
but I put *my fingers* in my ears.

syllabary: … *ᎤᏲᎸᎩ* ᏗᏜᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎻᎳ ᎡᎳᏫᏃ, ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎢᏳᏓᎨᏛ.
english: … *bearing* a mixture of myrrh and aloes, as it were, a hundred pounds.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎠᎾᏥ ᏚᏬᎡᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎠᏴᏓᏆᎶᏍᎩ* ᏧᏪᏥ ᎦᏛᎦ;
english: … and he put on them names — Boanerges, that is, ‘Sons of *thunder*;’
“He *was walking* in the fields.”
… and then when it is shined on by the sun the water *is dried by* the sun.

syllabary: ᏂᎦᎥᏰᏃ ᏗᎧᎿᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏚᎵᏁᏨ ᏌᏉᏉ ᎢᎧᏁᏨᎯ *ᎠᎧᎵᎢᎭ*, ᎯᎠᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎾᎥ ᎢᏗᏍᏓᏓᎳ ᎯᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏨᏒ ᏂᏣᏓᎨᏳᏒᎢ.
english: for all the law in one word is *fulfilled* — in this: ‘Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself;’

syllabary: ᎥᎵᏃ, ᏧᏓ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎡᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏍᎦᎾᎯᏳ ᎨᏎ ᏱᎰᏩ *ᏓᎧᏅᎢ*; ᏱᎰᏩᏃ ᎤᎴᎢ.
english: and Er, Judah's first-born, is evil in the *eyes* of Jehovah, and Jehovah doth put him to death.
The dogs *are chasing* the boy.

syllabary: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏩᏛᎲ ᎠᎩᎾ ᏐᏈᎵ-ᏗᎦᎵᎠᏅᎯᏛ, *ᎤᎩᎸᏅᎩ*, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ;
english: and Jesus having found a young ass *did sit upon it*, according as it is written,
‘Blind guides! who are straining out the gnat, and the camel *are swallowing*.
Truly, said Christian, *I do not know*.

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ *ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒᎩ*; ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎦᏙᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏬᎵᏤ ᏥᏌ ᎨᏒᎢ.
english: and these things having said, *she turned backward*, and seeth Jesus standing, and she had not known that it is Jesus.

syllabary: ᏱᎰᏩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎨᏂ, ᎭᏢ ᎡᏣᏅᏟ ᎡᏈᎵ? ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, Ꮭ ᏱᏥᎦᏔᎭ: ᏥᎪ ᎠᏴ ᎥᎩᏅᏟ *ᏥᎦᏘᏯ*?
english: And Jehovah saith unto Cain, 'Where [is] Abel thy brother?' and he saith, 'I have not known; my brother's *keeper -- I*?'
“It had *eyes* lifted up to Heaven.”

syllabary: ᏗᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᎧᎵᏎᏥ ᏚᏩᏎᎢ ᏧᏂᏍᏗᎩᏍᏗ. $1.25 ᏚᏈᏴᎮᎢ. ᏅᎩ ᏚᏩᏎᎢ. ᎲᎦ ᎢᎦ *ᏚᏈᏴᎮᎢ* ᏂᎦᏓ?  [ᏣᎳᎩ ᏅᎩᏁ ᏗᏂᏂᏙᎯ ᏗᏕᏲᏙᏗ]
phonetic: dideyohvsgi kalisetsi duwaseɂi tsunisdigisdi. $1.25 duquiyvheɂi. nvgi duwaseɂi. hvga iga *duquiyvheɂi* nigada?
english: The teacher bought candy for eating. He paid $1.25. He bought four. How much *did he pay* for all of them?
Hast thou a Wife *and* Children?

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ *ᎤᎴᏅᏔᏅᎩ* ᏥᏌ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎨᏂ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, …
english: This *beginning of* the signs did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, …

syllabary: ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ, *ᏥᏯᎵᎡᎵᏤᎭ* ᎠᏆᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎬᏗᎭ ᏚᏙᎥ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ …
english: first, indeed, *I thank* my God through Jesus Christ …
the which he took up and *rejoiced* therein …
… and partly *having become partners of* those so living,

syllabary: ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏆᏓᏅᏩᏗᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎯᎠ ᎦᎨᏗᏳ *ᏥᎦᎵᏎᎭ*.
english: I cannot go so fast as I would, by reason of this Burden *that is upon my back*.

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ *ᎤᎵᏰᏅ* ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏅ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏲ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏤᎢ.
english: and when the *herb sprang up*, and yielded fruit, then appeared also the darnel.
*He kicked* the dog.

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ ᎠᏥᏝᏁᎴ *ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ*; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, *ᎥᏝ ᏴᎦᎦᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᎦ* ᎬᏂ ᏥᏃᏅᎭ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎩᏃᎮᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ …
english: and setteth before him *to eat*; but he saith, '*I do not eat* till I have spoken my word;' …
The women *are dancing*.
… and *proclaiming* the good news …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏯᎾᏛᎬᎦ ᎩᎶ *ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎩ* ᏄᏁᎲᎾ ᏱᎩ?
english: … and how shall they hear apart from *one preaching*?
These things saying, *he spat* on the ground …
…and find there *a well* of living water,

syllabary: ᎾᏂᎥᏰᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎬᏗ ‾*ᎠᎹ* ᎨᏥᏅᏝᏰᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᏥᎸᎨᎳᏍᏗ ‾*ᎠᎹ* ᎦᏅᎵᏰᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
english: for every one with fire shall be *salted*, and every sacrifice with *salt* shall be salted.
Again, as thou sawest the Damsel sprinkle the room with *Water*.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎤᏴᏓᏆᎶᎥᎩ, ᎠᎴ *ᎠᎾᎦᎵᏍᎬᎩ*, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎵᏖᎸᏅᎩ.
english: … and thunders, and *lightnings*, and an earthquake.
and his countenance was as *lightning* …

syllabary: ᎤᎶᎩᎸ ᏑᏓᎵ *ᎠᏂ* ᏚᎨᎢ, ᏔᎵᏚ ᏚᎯᏰ. ᎭᎳᎢᎦ ᎨᏎ ᏂᎦᏓ *ᎠᏂ*?
english: Six *strawberries* were eaten, twelve remained. How many were all the *strawberries*?
and this they said, *trying him* …

syllabary: … ᏇᎵᏲ ᎠᏴ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏣᏁᏨᎾ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ *ᎤᏌᎳᏙᏗ* ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ.
english: … 'I [am] Pharaoh, and without thee a man doth not *lift up* his hand and his foot in all the land of Egypt;'

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏧᏔᏏᏅᎯ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎢᏗᏢ *ᎤᏌᎳᏓᏅᎩ* ᎤᏬᏰᏂ,
english: … and upon the land, *did lift up* his hand to the heaven,
Fear followed me so *hard* …

syllabary: ᎠᏂᏧᏏᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ *ᎤᏂᏍᏓᏱᏛᎩ*, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏗ, ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎩ.
english: and the Jews also *agreed*, professing these things to be so.
… and to whom He willeth, *He doth harden*.
that a man came to him, whose name was *Help* …
*He begged them* to get off of that turtle. (Spade/Walker 1966)

syllabary: ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ *ᎠᏂᏍᏙᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᏙᏍᎩᏱ*, ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎠᏥᏯᏅᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏐᎢᏃ ᎠᏥᎧᎯᏯᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
english: two women *shall be grinding in the mill*, one is received, and one is left.

syllabary: … ᏞᏍᏗ *ᏣᏍᏚᏅᎩ* ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏒᎯ ᎠᏂ ᎯᎠ ᎪᏪᎵᎯ …
english: … ‘Thou mayest not *seal* the words of the prophecy of this scroll …
… did come to *the borders* of Judea, beyond the Jordan,
… and darkness [is] on the face of *the deep* …
*I am directed by* a man, whose name is Evangelist …
… until that he hath ceased *to number*, for there is no number.
*but* he will have all now.

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵ ᎬᏩᎰᏅᎯ ᎦᏙᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᎾᏫᏍᎪ *ᎠᏍᎦᎢᎲᎢ*.
english: Here therefore he sweat and did quake *for fear*.
But may *my sin* be forgiven?
The *Man* answered.
… to be tempted by *the Devil*,

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏍᏚᎶ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏓᎶᏂᎨᏍᏛᎭ *ᎠᏍᎪᎵ* ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏢ ᏥᎦᏔ …
english: … and that a Crown hangs over *his head* …

syllabary: ᎠᏕᏗᏱᏍᎩᏂ, ᎠᏲᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏓᎯᏲᎩ, ᎠᎴ *ᎠᏍᎪᎸᎩ* ᏂᎨᏒᎾ …
english: I seek an Inheritance incorruptible, undefiled, and that *fadeth* not away …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏃᏁᎴ ᎠᎹ ᎠᏢᏗᏱ *ᎤᎾᏍᎪᏒ* ᎤᎬᏩᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎠᎹ ᎣᏥᏁᏩᏛ.
english: … the circumstances of the well which *they have digged*, and say to him, 'We have found water;'
… and bring thy hand, and put [it] to *my side* …
At this his Relations were sore *amazed* …

syllabary: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏎ *ᎠᏂᏏᎾᏌᏅᎬᎢ*, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏍᎩᏌᏛᎥᏍᎦ?
english: And he, having perceived *their craftiness*, said unto them, ‘Why me do ye tempt?

syllabary: … ᏄᎳ ᎭᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓ ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏟᎶᎥᎯ ᏩᎾᎨ ᎢᏒ; *ᎭᏑᎬᎵ*, ᎠᎴ ᏘᏃᏢᏛᎦ.
english: … 'Hasten three measures of flour-meal, *knead*, and make cakes;'
… for what cause *she touched him* declared to him before …

syllabary: … ᏞᏍᏗᏉ *ᎩᎾᏘᏲᎸᎩ* ᎠᏴ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎾᏝᎢ ᏗᎨᎩᏂᎦᏘᏕᎯ ᏞᏍᏗ *ᎤᎾᏘᏲᎸᎩ*; ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟᏉᏰᏃ.
english: … 'Let there not, I pray thee, be *strife* between me and thee, and between my shepherds and thy shepherds, for we [are] men -- brethren.
… and let them rule over *fish* of the sea …

syllabary: ᎬᏲᏎᎭ, ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎾᎩ *ᏣᏤᏍᏙ*, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏤᏅᏒ ᏫᎶᎯ.
english: I say to thee, Rise, and take up *thy couch*, and go away to thy house;’
The food is cooking in the pot in *the fire*.

syllabary: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂᏨᏍᏛᎦ *ᎠᏨᏍᏙᏗ* ᎠᏟᎶᏍᏗ ᏯᏄᏢᏗᏍᎪᎢ …
english: nor do they light *a lamp*, and put it under the measure …

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; Ꭷ, *ᎢᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓ* ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏆᏍᎩᏓᏒᎢ.
english: And he saith unto them, '*Hear* ye, I pray you, this dream which I have dreamed:
‘A voice in Ramah was *heard* …

syllabary: ᏥᏌ ᎤᏁᏨᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ *ᏥᏁᎸᎭ* ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏊᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ. ᎤᏭᏅᏃ ᎠᎬᎭᎸᏛ *ᎤᏁᎸᎩ* ᏧᏓᏏ ᎢᏍᎦᎳᏗ ᏌᏩᏂ ᎤᏪᏥ.
english: Jesus answereth, ‘That one it is to whom I, having dipped the morsel, *shall give it*;’ and having dipped the morsel, *he giveth [it]* to Judas of Simon, Iscariot.
… since himself also is compassed with *infirmity*;
… and ye know him, because he doth remain with you, and *shall be in* you.
and *called after me* to turn again.
… 'Let an expanse be *in the midst* of the waters …
… and *take up* his cross, and follow me,
… and the Writing is not able to *be broken*,
Rachel is cooking for *the children*.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎤᏲᎵᎸᎢ*.
english: … and was reasoning of what kind this *salutation* may be.

syllabary: … *ᎢᏥᏲᏍᏓ* ᎯᎠ ᎤᏛᏅᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏦᎢᏉᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏓᏥᏱᎵᏙᎵ ᎿᏉ ᏛᎦᏁᏍᎨᎰᏂ.
english: … ‘*Destroy* this sanctuary, and in three days I will raise it up.’
And *I*, lo, I am bringing in the deluge of waters on the earth …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏭᏗᏅᏒᎩ; ᏚᏃᏴᎳᏒᎩᏃ, ᎠᎴ *ᎤᏴᏓᏆᎶᎥᎩ*, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎦᎵᏍᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎵᏖᎸᏅᎩ.
english: … and did cast [it] to the earth, and there came voices, and *thunders*, and lightnings, and an earthquake.
… may not *enter* into it.’

syllabary: … ᏧᏂᏁᎦᎸᏅᎯ ᏚᏪᏰᏑᎳᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏯᏢᏁ ᏓᏫᏍᎦᎨ ᎨᏒ *ᎠᏴᏤᏂ*;
english: … she hath put on his hands, and on *the smooth of his neck*,
… and his testimony none *receiveth*;

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ *ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ*, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᎪᏎᎮᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
english: … but whoever may do and may *teach [them]*, he shall be called great in the reign of the heavens.
“*Turtle*, where is that pretty sound coming from?”

syllabary: … ᏦᏩᏃ ᏚᏁᎴ *ᏓᏆᎴᎷ* ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᏁᏨ ᏇᎵᏲ, …
english: … and Joseph giveth *waggons* to them by the command of Pharaoh, …

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒ, *ᏚᎵᏂᏆᏅᏅᎩ* ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
english: And these things having said, *having bowed his knees*, with them all, he did pray,
… every one who *is looking* on a woman to desire her, …
… *Loose the sandal of thy feet*, for the place in which thou hast stood is holy ground;

syllabary: *ᏔᎴᎲᎦ*, ᎮᏓ ᏂᎦᏅᎯᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏯᏛᎥ ᎯᎠ ᎦᏙᎯ; ᏂᎯᏰᏃ ᏓᎬᏁᎵ.
english: *rise*, go up and down through the land, to its length, and to its breadth, for to thee I give it.'
The man *is drawing* and painting the picture.

syllabary: … ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎡᎦᏔ-ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏡᎬ ᎤᏫᏎ ᏖᎸᎳᏗ *ᏚᏫᏒᎢ*; …
english: …‘A certain one had a fig-tree *planted* in his vineyard, …
… is any of good cheer? let him *sing* psalms;

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎤᎷᏨ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ *ᏚᏪᏲᏅᎩ*; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏒᎩ, …
english: and having come to his own country, *he was teaching them* in their synagogue, so that they were astonished, …

syllabary: ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᎷᏤ ᏣᏂ *ᏗᏓᏬᏍᎩ* ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏧᏗᏱ,
english: And in those days cometh John the *Baptist*, proclaiming in the wilderness of Judea,

syllabary: … ᎦᏙᏃ *ᏗᏤᏲᎲᏍᎩ* ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏂᎩᏏᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎾ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎢᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎦ?
english: … ‘Wherefore with the tax-gatherers and sinners doth *your teacher* eat?’

syllabary: *ᏗᏕᎶᏆᏍᎩ* ᎥᏝ ᎤᏟ ᏱᎾᏥᎸᏉᏙᎢ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏧᏪᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏟ ᏱᎾᏥᎸᏉᏙᎢ ᏒᏍᎦᏉ ᎤᏅᏏᏙᎯ.
english: ‘*A disciple* is not above the teacher, nor a servant above his lord;

syllabary: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ *ᏗᎪᏪᎵᏍᎩ* ᎤᎷᏤᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏔᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᏓᎬᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎵ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎮᏙᎲᎢ.
english: and a certain *scribe* having come, said to him, ‘Teacher, I will follow thee wherever thou mayest go;’

syllabary: ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᏁᎯ. ᎭᏑᏯᎩ. ᏫᎦᎯᏁᎥᏏ: Ꭰ) ᎧᏫ, Ꭱ) ᏌᎶᎵ, Ꭲ) *ᏗᎵ*.
english: He is holding something. Choose it. Hand him: a) Coffee, b) Squirrels, c) *Skunks*.
“Lots of *gnats*”, she was saying as she was being hit by the bullets.

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ [ᎠᎨᏴ] ᎿᏉ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎤᏂᏴ ᎤᏄᏬ ᎤᏒᎦᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ *ᏙᏱ* ᏮᏗᏢᏍᏔᏅᎢ,
english: And it cometh to pass when she seeth that he hath left his garment in her hand, and fleeth *without*,
“I want *to work* and rest at home.”

syllabary: ᏥᏌ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ, ᎯᎾᎩ ᏣᏤᏍᏙ, ᎠᎴ *ᎮᏓ*.
english: Jesus saith to him, ‘Rise, take up thy couch, and *be walking*;’
You and Mary *go* to the store!

syllabary: ᏗᏂᏲᎵᏃ ᎨᎬᏂᏍᏓᏁ ᏎᎷ ᏃᎴ ᏚᏯ *ᎡᏆ* ᏧᎳᏍᎩ ᎬᏗ ᎡᏥᏂ.
english: Rachel is cooking the corn and beans in a *large* pot for the children.
… and riseth, and passeth over the *River*, …
Bears, deer, and bobcats *live in* the wild.

syllabary: … ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎠᏆᏓᎵᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏇᏥ ᏂᎦᎥ *ᎡᎯᏍᏗ* …
english: … that his Wife and Children should not perceive his *distress*, …

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ *ᎡᎳᏗ* ᏕᎤᎳᏍᎬ ᎾᎥ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎢᏗᎬᏤᎵᏛ ᎤᏅᏥᎸᎩ, ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎩ …
english: Then Christian fell *down* at his foot as dead, crying …
for *it doth seem to me* irrational …

syllabary: ᎦᏙᏃ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏣᏲᎴᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎦᏗ *ᎡᎶᎯ* ᏥᏕᏣᏲᏍᎦ?
english: What are the things you seek, since you leave all *the world* to find them?
Color the ninth *flag* red.
… He who is eating the bread with me, did lift up against me *his heel*.
“*What* are you doing?”

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎠᎵᏣᏙᏂᎪᎯ, *ᎯᏙᎨᏍᏗ* ᏞᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏓᎬᏃᏁᎵ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎯ. ᎾᏍᎩᏃ *ᎦᏙᎪ* ᎤᏪᎾᏫᏍᎪᎢ.
english: Then, said Evangelist, *stand* still a little, that I may shew thee the words of God. So *he stood* trembling.

syllabary: ᏤᎦᏈᏃ *ᎦᏚ* ᎠᎴ ᏚᏯ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᎫᏅᎯ ᏚᏁᎴ ᎢᏐ, ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏴᏁᏃ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏗᏔᎮᎢ, …
english: and Jacob hath given to Esau *bread* and pottage of lentiles, and he eateth, and drinketh, …
Help the goat arrive high *up the mountain*.

syllabary: … ᏂᎯ ᏦᏰᏂ ᎨᏣᏍᎦᎩ *ᎠᏂᎩᎵᎨᏂ* ᏕᎯᏂᏴᏎᏍᏗ, ᏣᏙᏓ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏂᎨᏣᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ.
english: … Thy hand [is] on *the neck* of thine enemies, Sons of thy father bow themselves to thee.
They made *a wedge* and maul from locust.

syllabary: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᏅᏲᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎳᎨᏯᏛᏤᎢ, ᎦᏲᎵ *ᎦᏓ* ᎦᎳᎨᏴᎢ, ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎵᏰᏁᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏌᎨᎢᏳ ᎨᏒ *ᎦᏓ*.
english: and others fell upon the rocky places, where they had not much *earth*, and immediately they sprang forth, through not having depth of *earth*,

syllabary: ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᎠᏯᎡ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ *ᎦᏓᎭ* ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏯᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᎷᏁ,
english: And there was in their synagogue a man with an *unclean* spirit, and he cried out,

syllabary: … ᏦᏩ *ᎦᎵᏆᏚ* ᎢᏳᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎨᏎ ᏧᏂᎵ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎭ ᏓᏅᎿᏗᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎠᏫ; …
english: … Joseph, a son of *seventeen* years, hath been enjoying himself with his brethren among the flock, …

syllabary: … ᎡᏍᎦᏉ *ᎦᎵᏉᎩ* ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎿᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎬᏩᏃᏏᏐᏗ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ.
english: … are wiser in their own eyes than *seven* men that can render a reason.
The man that was *sleeping*, was dreaming.​

syllabary: … ᎴᎻᎩ [ᎤᎸᎯ] *ᎦᎵᏆᏍᎪᎯ* ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ ᎠᏎ ᎠᎦᏞᏤᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
english: … And for Lamech *seventy* and sevenfold.'

syllabary: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏫᏚᎾᏙᏯᏅᎯᏕ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᎬᏩᏎᏒᎮ ᎶᏗ *ᎦᎵᏦᏕ* ᎬᏩᏴᏔᏁ ᎠᏂᏯᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏚᏁ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
english: And the men put forth their hand, and bring in Lot unto them, into *the house*, and have shut the door;
The flower *vase* is empty.
… and their destruction *doth not slumber*.

syllabary: ᎭᏢ ᎤᏂᎦᏔ ᏓᏰᎮ ᎩᏳᎩ? Ꭰ) *ᏗᎦᏃᎨᏂ*, Ꭱ) ᎠᏳᎨᏂ.
english: Where does a chipmunk keep his seeds? a) In *his arms*, b) In his cheeks.
Issacher [is] a strong ass, *Crouching* between the two folds;
Yet at the usual way on the bridge! Not *a thing* happened to us.
for each one his own burden *shall bear*.

syllabary: … ᏕᎦᏰᏌᏛ *ᏗᎦᎴᏂ* ᏚᏐᎾᏕᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎵᏥᏍᏇᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᎪ ᎤᏒᏂᎴᎢ;
english: … he put his fingers to *his ears*, and having spit, he touched his tongue,

syllabary: … ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᎿᏉ ᏦᎢ ᎾᏕᏘᏴ ᏥᎷᎬᎩ ᎠᎩᏲᎲᎩ ᎤᏓᏔᏅᎯ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎦᏔ-ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏡᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᏩᏘᎭ; *ᎯᎴᏴᏍᏓ*; …
english: … Lo, three years I come seeking fruit in this fig-tree, and do not find, *cut it off*, …

syllabary: ᎢᏨᎨᏳᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏱᏥᏰᎸᏎᏍᏗ *ᎦᎵᎬ* ᏥᏥᏱᎵᏕᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏥᎪᎵᏰᏗ ᏥᎩ, …
english: Beloved, think it not strange at the *fiery* suffering among you that is coming to try you, …
Then, therefore, did Pilate take Jesus and *scourge* [him],

syllabary: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎦᏙᎯ *ᏕᎯᎶᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ*, ᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎥᏝ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏱᏣᏛᎯᏎᎮᏍᏗ; …
english: when *thou tillest* the ground, it doth not add to give its strength to thee …

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏒ ᎠᎵᏣᏙᏂᏙᎯ. (ᏭᏐᏅ Ꭰ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ) “ᏫᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪ Ꮎ ᎬᎾᏙᎵ *ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗ* ᏨᏗᎪᏢᎭ?” ᎠᏍᎦᏯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, “ᎥᏝ”
english: Then said Evangelist, pointing with his finger over a very wide field, “Do you see yonder Wicket-*gate*?” The Man said, “No.”
Crane *is being passed by* Hummingbird.
“*I am chopping it* with a knife.”
… my ministration *I do glorify*;

syllabary: … ᎠᏎᏃ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎣᏓᎷᎶᏗ ᏭᎷᏥᎸ ᎣᏣᏗᏉ *ᎦᎸᎳᏗ* ᏂᎦᏙ ᏅᏩᏍᏙᎢ …
english: … but behold, when he was got now hard by the Hill, it seemed so *high* …
In the beginning of God's preparing *the heavens* and the earth --

syllabary: ᎫᎫ ᎭᏫᎾᏗᏜ *ᎯᎸᎥᎦ* ᎪᏪᎵ. ᏃᏭᏃ ᎠᎹ ᎬᏗ ᎯᎧᎵᏣ.
english: *Put* the paper into the jar. And then fill with water.
… *take up* thy couch, and go to thy house.’
“*He lives* near me.”
*The doctor* investigates your eyes.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ *ᎦᏁᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ* ᎠᎹ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᏓᏁᎯ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
english: … and he who is willing — let him *take* the water of life freely.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏣᏗ *ᏕᎦᏁᎭ*; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏎᏉᎧᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎯᎠᏃ ᏥᏄᏂᏣᏔ?
english: … who *hath* … two fishes, but these — what are they to so many?’

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ ᏥᏌ *ᎦᏁᏥᎢ* ᎤᏍᏛᎦᎵ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎦᎪ?
english: and that one having leant back on *the breast* of Jesus, respondeth to him, ‘Sir, who is it?’

syllabary: Ꭷ, *ᏘᏄᎦ* ᏗᏣᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏗ, ᎦᏃᏟ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎶᏣᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᏫᎶᎯ, ᎠᏫᏃ ᎤᏫᏯ ᏍᎩᏲᏎᎷᎦ;
english: and now, *take up*, I pray thee, thy instruments, thy quiver, and thy bow, and go out to the field, and hunt for me provision,
*he*, therefore, *who is despising* — doth not *despise* man, …
No one knows the whereabouts of *his burial*.

syllabary: … ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎠᎵᏣᏙᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎦᏗᏏ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ *ᎤᏂᏴᎲᎩ*, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ, …
english: … At the sight of which, Evangelist *caught* him by the right hand, saying, …
… Thou mayest not *steal*, …

syllabary: … ᏗᏣᎴᎲᎦ ᎠᎴ *ᎢᏥᏄᎪᎢ* ᎠᏂ; ᏱᎰᏩᏰᏃ ᏛᏲᏍᏔᏂ ᎯᎠ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ. …
english: … 'Rise, *go out from* this place, for Jehovah is destroying the city;' …

syllabary: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏂᎦᎥ *ᎦᏄᎸᎯ* ᎠᎦᏔᏛᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏣᏛᏍᎦ, …
english: And God saith, 'Lo, I have given to you every *herb* sowing seed, which [is] upon the face of all the earth, …

syllabary: … ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏌᏉ ᎡᎳᏗ *ᎤᏅᎢᏍᏗ* ᏱᎩ ᎢᏥᏙᏓ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
english: … and one of them shall not *fall on the ground* without your Father;
And as he is going forth into *the way* …
and if a fish he may ask — a serpent *will he present to him*?
… they did not break *his legs*;
… he is not here; lo, the place where *they laid him*!

syllabary: *ᎦᏌᎴᎾ* ᏠᏯ ᏨᏛᎩᏂᏴᎩ ᎦᏆ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᏖᏒ ᏘᏁᏒᎭ, …
english: *the cloak* that I left in Troas with Carpus, coming, …
… and *the tables* he overthrew,
“*I will speak* in Cherokee.”

syllabary: ᏤᏂ ᏓᎳᏚ *ᏗᎦᏯᎸᏗ* ᏚᏩᏎᎢ. ᎤᎪᎮᎢ ᏔᎵ ᎢᎦᎢ *ᏗᎦᏯᎸᏗ* ᎬᎿᎨ ᎤᏍᎪᎸᎯ ᏕᎨᏒᎢ. ᎭᎳ ᎢᎦ *ᏗᎦᏯᎸᏗ* ᏄᏓᎴ ᏧᎵᏑᏫᏓ ᏕᎨᏎᎢ?
english: John bought twelve *postage stamps*. He saw that two *postage stamps* were grey. How many *postage stamps* were differently colored?
*She is sewing* pants and dresses.

syllabary: *ᎦᏲᎵ* ᎠᎹ ᏩᏂᏁᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏣᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᏗᏦᏑᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏣᏪᏐᎸᏍᏓ ᏡᎬ ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢ;
english: let, I pray thee, *a little* water be accepted, and wash your feet, and recline under the tree;
Thou hast met with something (as I perceive) *already*; …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ *ᏕᎨᎶᎰᎢ*. ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏟ ᏁᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᏂᎯ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏥᏍᏆ.
english: … and God *doth nourish them*; how much better are ye than the fowls?

syllabary: … ᏩᎦ ᎠᏂᎩᎾ *ᎤᏂᎩᎬ* ᎠᎴ ᎠᏫ ᏗᏂᎭᏄᎸᎯ *ᎤᏂᎩᎬ* ᎤᏁᎩᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏅ ᎩᎦᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏐᏈ, ᏚᏍᏚᏞᏃ ᎪᏪᎵ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ.
english: … having taken *the blood* of the calves and goats, with water, and scarlet wool, and hyssop, he both the book itself and all the people did sprinkle,

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏔᏅ *ᎩᎦᎨ* ᎢᎾᏛ [ᏫᏥᎪᎥᎩ,] ᎦᎸᏉᎩ ᏓᏍᎫᏓᏛᎩ, …
english: … and, lo, a great *red* dragon, having seven heads …
He will kick *the dog*.

syllabary: … ᎤᏩᏂᎦᎸ ᎿᏉ ᎤᏓᎨ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎦᎵᏍᏚᎲᏍᎪᎢ, ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎰ *ᎪᎩ* ᎾᎥᏂᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ;
english: … when the branch may already become tender, and may put forth the leaves, ye know that nigh is *the summer*;

syllabary: … ᎾᏍᎩᏃ *ᎪᎯ* ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎸᏏᎸ ᎤᏛᏛᏅᎩ.
english: … who *after a little time* came to Christian, and asked him what he would have?

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᎥᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎨᏳᏒᎩ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᏕᎤᏲᏒᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏒᎯᏳ ᎤᏕᏁᎸᎯ, ᎩᎶ ᎾᏍᎩ *ᏱᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ* ᎤᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎬᏂᏛᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏩᏛᏗ.
english: for God did so love the world, that His Son — the only begotten — He gave, that every one who *is believing* in him may not perish, but may have life age-during.

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᎦᎵᏒᎩ ᎠᏍᏕᏱᏛ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏥᏳ ᎦᏙᏌᏗᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎤᏬᎰᏒᎩ*.
english: then the soldiers did cut off the ropes of the boat, and *suffered it to fall off*.
All who are learning are studying *the book*.

syllabary: … ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏗᏌᏓᏛᎩ, ᎦᏰᏌᏛᏃ ᎤᏩᏔᏅᎩ *ᎤᏬᏪᎳᏅᎩ* ᎦᏙᎯ.
english: … And Jesus, having stooped down, with the finger *he was writing* on the ground,
… and maketh it a standing pillar, and poureth *oil* upon its top,

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏔᏲᎯᎮᏍᏗ *ᎪᎳ* ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
english: and pray ye that your flight may not be *in winter*, nor on a sabbath;
… neither do *I understand* what thou sayest …

syllabary: ᎠᏏᏴᏫᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏅᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏅᏃᏛ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏚᏁᎸᎩ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ *ᎫᎫ* ᎠᏕᎸᏓᎶᏂᎨ ᏗᎪᏢᏔᏅᎯ, …
english: and one of the four living creatures did give to the seven messengers seven golden *vials*, …
Help the squirrel walk his *acorns* to where they go.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ *ᎫᎴᏗᏍᎪᏂᎯ* ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᏧᏠᎠᏏᏗᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏪᏯᎸᏤᎢ.
english: … and the Spirit as *a dove* coming down upon him;
Give me thy hand: so he gave him his hand, and he *drew him out*, …
They made a wedge and maul *from* locust.
The food *is cooking*.

syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎧᏁ? ᎭᏑᏯᎩ. *ᏫᏘᏯᎧᏏ*: Ꭰ) ᎬᏃᏌᏍᏗ, Ꭱ) ᎦᏓ, Ꭲ) ᏥᏍᏕᏥ.
english: What is seen? Choose. Give him them: a) *Brooms*, b) Dirt, c) Rats.

syllabary: … ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᏍᎩᎳᏏᏘᏍᎨᏍᏗ, *ᎠᏂ* ᎯᎠ ᎪᏪᎵᎯ ᎯᎪᎵᏯ …
english: … If you believe not me, read *here* in this Book …
“*Alright*!”, yelled Al.
Then they put dry wood *inside*.

syllabary: ᎤᏟ ᎤᏏᏳ *ᎭᏫᏯ* ᎠᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, …
english: Right [it is] not to eat *flesh*, nor to drink wine, …

syllabary: ᎡᏆᎭᎻᏃ ᎤᏙᏯᏅᎯᏕ ᎠᎴ *ᎭᏰᎳᏍᏗ* ᎤᏴᎮ ᎤᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎤᏪᏥ.
english: and Abraham putteth forth his hand, and taketh *the knife* -- to slaughter his son.
At *this* his Relations were sore amazed; …

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᎱᎩᏍᎬ ᎧᏁᏉᏥᏎ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ *ᎯᏍᎦᏍᎪᎯ* ᏧᏒᎯᏛ.
english: and the waters are mighty on the earth a hundred and *fifty* days.
And out of his brethren he hath taken *five* men, …
“*When* are you coming back?”

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎢᏍᏔᏁ ᎤᏂᏁᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏏ *ᎢᎦ* ᏂᎬᏒᎾ ᏦᏩ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏁᏰᏃ ᎾᎿ ᏗᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏂᏒᎢ.
english: and they prepare the present until the coming of Joseph at *noon*, for they have heard that there they do eat bread.

syllabary: ᎾᏍᏉᏃ *ᎥᎤᎷᏨᎩ* ᏂᎦᏗᎹᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᏥᏌ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎤᎷᏤᎸᎯ …
english: and Nicodemus also *came* — who came unto Jesus by night at the first …
*during* all days of the earth … do not cease.'

syllabary: ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨᏃ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏏ *ᎢᏅᎯᏳ* ᏱᏩᎾᎢᏎᎢ, …
english: they have gone out of the city -- they have not gone *far off* …
Yes, *both* Obstinate and Pliable;
… did inquire exactly from them *the time of* the appearing star,
Then, as it burned it popped and made a whistling-*like* sound.

syllabary: … *ᎢᏳᏍᏗᏉ* ᎮᎵᏍᎬ ᏍᎩᏔᏲᏏ, ᏓᎬᏁᎵᏃ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎾᎿ ᎠᎩᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
english: … ‘*Whatever* thou mayest ask me, I will give to thee — unto the half of my kingdom.’
“*Who* was that?”

syllabary: ᏗᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ *ᎧᎵᏎᏥ* ᏚᏩᏎᎢ ᏧᏂᏍᏗᎩᏍᏗ. $1.25 ᏚᏈᏴᎮᎢ. ᏅᎩ ᏚᏩᏎᎢ. ᎭᎳ ᎢᎦ ᏚᏈᏴᎮᎢ ᏂᎦᏓ?
english: The teacher bought *candy* for eating. He paid $1.25. He bought four. How much did he pay for all of them?
They made a wedge and maul from *locust*.

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏎ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎢᎩᏛ ᏠᎨᏏ, ᎾᎿ *ᎤᎵᏦᏔᏅᎢ*, ᎮᎼ ᏧᏪᏥ ᏚᏩᎯᏎᎴᎢ, …
english: and he buyeth the portion of the field where he hath *stretched out his tent*, …
*A spider* has 8 legs to stay up with.

syllabary: ᎴᏆᏂᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎰᏩᏉ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ *ᏣᏁᏨᎢ*.
english: And Laban saith, 'Lo, O that it were according to *thy word*;'
How much money is piled up in each *box*?

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎤᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏧᎳ *ᎧᏁᏍᎦ* ᎠᎴ ᎨᎶᎸᏗ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎣᎩᎭ, …
english: She saith also unto him, 'Both *straw* and provender [are] abundant with us, …

syllabary: … ᎠᏤᎷᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ *ᏗᎧᏃᎩᏍᏙᏗ* ᏱᎩ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏧᏓᎴᎿᎢ ᏂᏚᏃᏴᎬᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎦᏙ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᏴᏙᎴᎰᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏤᎷᎯᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ *ᏗᎧᏃᎩᏛ* ᎨᏒᎢ?
english: … whether pipe or *harp* — if a difference in the sounds they may not give, how shall be known that which is piped or that which is *harped*?

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎪᎲᎢ ᎤᏛᎦᏅᎾᏍᎩ *ᎧᏃᎮᎭ*, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏱᏓᏓᏂᎸᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎤᏃᎮᎸᎢ*.
english: ‘And what he hath seen and heard this *he doth testify*, and *his testimony* none receiveth;

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ *ᎤᏅᏩᏅᎩ*; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏗᎨᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᎨᏫ ᎤᏬᏂᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲᎩ.
english: … and *he healed him*, so that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂ ᏤᏥ ᎤᎷᏦᏅᎯᏉ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏒᏁᎸᎯ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎠᏂᎨᏴᎯ ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ *ᎤᏪᎳᏗᏙᎸᎢ*, ᎢᎯᎡᎸ ᎦᎵᏦᏔᏅᎯ ᏩᎦ ᎠᎩᎾ.
english: but when thy son — this one who did devour thy living *with* harlots — came, thou didst kill to him the fatted calf.

syllabary: ᎵᏇᎩᏃ ᏚᏌᎳᏓᏁ ᏗᎦᏙᎵ, ᏒᏏᎩᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ, ᎤᏠᎠᏎ *ᎨᎻᎵᎯ*.
english: And Rebekah lifteth up her eyes, and seeth Isaac, and alighteth from off *the camel*;

syllabary: … ᎠᏎᏃ *ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶᎢ*, ᎦᏙ ᏣᏲᎭ? ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᎯᏯᎵᏃᎮᏍᏗ? ᏳᏬᏎᎴᎢ.
english: … *no one*, however, said, ‘What seekest thou?’ or ‘Why speakest thou with her?’

syllabary: ᎴᏆᏂᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᏂᎯ ᎠᏴ *ᏗᎩᎪᎳ* ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᏇᏓᎸᎢ …
english: and Laban saith to him, 'Only *my bone* and my flesh [art] thou;' …

syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᏁᎭ? ᎭᏑᏯᎩ. ᏫᎦᎯᎥᏏ: Ꭰ) ᏥᏍᏛᎾ, Ꭱ) ᏙᏯ, Ꭲ) *ᏆᎾ*.
english: What was he handed? Choose. Hand him them: a) Crayfish, b) Beaver, c) *Peaches*.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᏓᎩᎧᎭ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎬᏆᏁᎶᏙᎢ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎮᎾ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᎡᎪᎢ; ᏅᏩᏓᎴᏃ ᎡᎮᎾ ᏥᏲᏏ, ᏗᎡᎪᎢ; ᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗᏃ, ᎯᎠ *ᎿᏛᎦ* ᏥᏲᏏ, ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎾᏛᏁᎰᎢ*.
english: … having under myself soldiers, and I say to this one, Go, and he goeth, and to another, Be coming, and he cometh, and to my servant, *Do this*, and *he doth* [it].’

syllabary: *ᎾᏉ* ᎠᏆᎴᏅᎲ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎦᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏎᎦᏥᏙᎸᎯ ᎠᏆᏓᏗᏫᏎᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
english: *Now* I begin to reap the benefits of my hazards.

syllabary: … ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᏄᏍᏗ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎪᎯᏉ ᎨᏒ ᏤᎾᎠ, ᎤᏇᎵᏃ ᎢᎦᏚᎸᎲᏗ ᎨᏒ *ᎾᎥᏂ* ᏂᏚᏓᎸ; ᎠᎴ …
english: … But though this be so, yet since things present and our fleshly appetite are such *near* neighbors one to another; and, …

syllabary: … ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎯᎾᎩ ᎪᏪᎵ ᎡᏣᏚᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ *ᏁᎳᏍᎪᎯ* ᎰᏪᎸᎦ.
english: … and he saith to him, Take thy bill, and write *eighty*.
And last, *everyone* ate warm, delicious bread.
… *saying* “What shall I do?”
and I knew that Thou *always* dost hear me, …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᏭᏃᎮᎵᏙᎴ *ᏂᎬᎢ* ᎦᏚᎲ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᏥᏌ.
english: … and he went away *through all* the city proclaiming how great things Jesus did to him.

syllabary: ᎴᏥᎵ ᏍᎪᎯ *ᏃᏈᏏ* ᏚᎪᎮᎢ ᏃᎴ ᏑᏓᎵ ᏫᏚᎪᎮ. ᎭᎳᎢᎦ *ᏃᏈᏏ* ᏚᎪᎮᎢ?
english: Lejili saw ten *meadowlarks* and then she saw six went away. How many *meadowlarks* does she see?
They made a wedge *and* maul from locust.

syllabary: ᏦᏩᏃ ᎤᏣᎴᏍᏗ ᎤᏪᏟᏌᏁ *ᏃᏳ* ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏥᎦᎳᎨᏲ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, …
english: and Joseph gathereth corn as *sand* of the sea, multiplying exceedingly, …
… hath become *four* chief [rivers];
… and their hap was to meet just as they were crossing *the way* of each other …
“You should be learning doctoring *medicine* …
Put inside seeds or little *rocks*.
The *rock armor* was ripped.

syllabary: ᎿᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᎾᎵᏒᎭ *ᎣᏓᎵ* ᏩᏂᎶᏒᎭ; …
english: then those in Judea, let them flee to *the mountains*; …
and Christian saw him *no more*.
It was burning *good*.
Yes, I see it *very well*.

syllabary: … *ᎣᏏᏳ* ᎦᏙᎯ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏴ ᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎨᏐ.”
english: … the ground is *good* when they are once got in at the Gate.

syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᏁᎭ? ᎭᏑᏯᎩ. ᏫᏥᎥᏏ: Ꭰ) ᏥᏍᏚ, Ꭱ) *ᏌᎶᎵ*, Ꭲ) ᎤᏪᏥ.
english: What is being handed? Choose. I am handing it to him: a) A rabbit, b) *A squirrel*, c) An egg.
A *hard*-back book $2.25, A soft-back book. $0.75.
“Come here *awhile* …

syllabary: ᎨᎯᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᏁᏙᎮ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ *ᏏᏆ* ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᏂᏙᎮᎢ.
english: And there was far off from them a herd of many *swine* feeding,

syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᏁᎭ? ᎭᏑᏯᎩ. ᏙᏗᏍᎩᏂᎥᏏ: Ꭰ) ᏪᏌ, Ꭱ) ᏅᏯ, Ꭲ) *ᏐᏈᎵ*.
english: What is he giving? Choose. He will hand them to him: a) Cats, b) Rocks, c) *Horses*.
The men became antagonized (as their number) became *one* less.

syllabary: … ᏝᏍᎪ ᎠᏍᎪᎯ ᏱᎨᏥᏅᎦᎸᎡᎢ? ᎠᏎᏃ *ᏐᎣᏁᎳ* ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎭᏢ?
english: … ‘Were not the ten cleansed, and *the nine* — where?

syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎢᏤᎵᎭ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᎠᏫ ᏱᏚᎾᏝᎠ, ᏌᏉᏃ ᏳᎴᏲᎡᎸ, ᏝᏍᎪ *ᏐᎣᏁᎳᏍᎪᎯ* ᏐᎣᏁᎳᎦᎵ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏱᏗᎬᏕᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏱᏗᎧᎾᎷᏏᏙᎰ ᎦᏚᏏ ᏳᏲᎰ ᎤᎴᏲᎡᎸᎯ?
english: ‘What think ye? if a man may have an hundred sheep, and there may go astray one of them, doth he not — having left the *ninety*-nine, having gone on the mountains — seek that which is gone astray?

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏰᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏛᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎧᏁᏉᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎦᏔᏔᏁᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛ ᏦᎠᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ *ᏑᏓᎵᏍᎪᎯ*, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ.
english: and other fell to the good ground, and was giving fruit, coming up and increasing, and it bare, one thirty-fold, and one *sixty*, and one an hundred.’
… for I have borne to him *six* sons;' and she calleth his name Zebulun;

syllabary: … ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᎠᎴ *ᏑᎾᎴᎢ* ᏓᏲᏤᏏ ᎨᎯ ᏗᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᏑᏕᏘᏴᏛᏃ ᏬᏤᎮᏍᏗ …
english: … ‘To-day and *to-morrow* we will go on to such a city, and will pass there one year, …

syllabary: ᏤᎦᏈᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏛᏁᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵ *ᏑᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᏗ* ᎨᏒ ᎤᏍᏆᏕᎴᎢ; …
english: And Jacob doth so, and fulfilleth *the week* of this one, …
*Two* geese are hearing the dog making music,

syllabary: … ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᏍᎩᏓᏍᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏦᎢ *ᏔᎷᏣ* ᎦᏚ ᎤᏂᏁᎬ ᏗᎦᎶᏗ ᏓᏆᏃᏍᏛᎩ;
english: … 'I also [am] in a dream, and lo, three *baskets* of white bread [are] on my head,
… gone up hath its blossom, its clusters have ripened *grapes*;
“Come here awhile, let me kill the *lice*”.
One *rabbit* is listening to the dog making music.

syllabary: ᎡᏘ ᎢᏳᏂᏪᏍᏗ ᏥᎨᏎ ᏌᏉ *ᏥᏍᏆ* ᏲᏒᎦᎸ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ …
english: That proverb, *A Bird* in the Hand is worth two in the Bush …

syllabary: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ *ᏥᏍᏆᏯ* ᏌᏉ ᎢᏯᏓᏅᏖᏗ ᏧᎾᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏱᎩ, …
english: ‘Are not two *sparrows* sold for an assar? …

syllabary: ᏦᎾᏰᏃ *ᏦᎢ* ᎢᎦ ᎠᎴ *ᏦᎢ* ᏒᏃᏱ ᎠᏣᏗ ᎡᏆ ᎤᏍᏉᎵᏱ ᏣᏯᎡᎢ, …
english: for, as Jonah was in the belly of the fish *three* days and *three* nights, …

syllabary: ᏗᎧᎸᎬᏃ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏗᏂᎶᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᏕᎵᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏴᏢᎢ, ᎠᎴ *ᏧᎦᎾᏮᎢ*, …
english: and they shall come from east and west, and from north and *south*, …
… and brought him a bag of *treasure*.

syllabary: ᏗᎧᎸᎬᏃ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏗᏂᎶᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᏕᎵᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ *ᏧᏴᏢᎢ*, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎦᎾᏮᎢ, …
english: and they shall come from east and west, and from *north* and south, …
‘*The foxes* have holes, and the birds of the heaven places of rest, …
The food is cooking in *the pot*.
Rachel is cooking *beans* and corn for the children.

syllabary: ᎥᏝ ᏌᏉ ᏱᎬᏩᏜᏍᏗ ᎡᎯᏍᏗ ᎢᏯᏓᏛᏁᎯ, *ᏧᎾᏓᎨᏳᎯᏉᏍᎩᏂ* ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᏍᎦᏅᎾ.
english: none of them are hurtful, *but loving* and holy.
Wherefore at length he brake his mind to *his Wife* and Children.

syllabary: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂ ᎧᏅᏑᎸ ᏴᏫ *ᎤᏓᏅᏙ* ᎦᏃᎮᏛ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏬᏢᎯᏌᏁᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
english: This parlour is *the heart* of a man that was never sanctified by the sweet Grace of the Gospel.

syllabary: ᎠᎨᏴᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᎢᎾᏛ; ᏰᎵᏉ ᎦᏲᎩᏂᎩᏍᏗ *ᎤᏓᏔᏅᎯ* ᎠᏫᏒᏗᏱ ᏥᏕᏡᎦ.
english: And the woman saith unto the serpent, 'Of *the fruit* of the trees of the garden we do eat,
… pluck it out and *cast from thee*, …
Sequoyah was born *in the year* 1767.
And Jesus *having been born in* Beth-Lehem of Judea, …
I have a baby *brother*. I am older than *my brother*.

syllabary: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᏂᏲᏎ ᏥᏳ ᎠᎴ *ᎤᏂᏙᏓ*, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ.
english: and they, immediately, having left the boat and *their father*, did follow him.
Groundhog, do you see *your shadow*?
The men *want* the women to cook.
One cannot eat a lot of *sweets* without the possible result of rotten teeth.
At first, *Owl* asked a question.

syllabary: *ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ*, ᎾᎿ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᏪᎳ ᎯᎠ ᎪᏪᎵᎯ …
english: *The Lord*, the Governor of the country, hath recorded that in this Book …

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏕᎬᏕᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥ, ᎤᏓᎵᎢᏃ ᎠᏜᏫᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏌᏉᏉ *ᎤᏂᏇᏓᎸᎢ* ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
english: therefore doth a man leave his father and his mother, and hath cleaved unto his wife, and they have become one *flesh*.
How much money *might you have*?
And his setting *thy feet* in that way that leadeth unto the administration of Death.

syllabary: ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ *ᎤᎾᏅᏓᏛᎩ* ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏅ ᎪᏪᎸᎢ; “ᏣᏤᎵ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᏓᏇᎷᎬ ᎠᎩᏯᎣᏅ.”
english: And his disciples *remembered* that it is written, ‘The zeal of Thy house did eat me up;’

syllabary: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏤᎲ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ *ᎤᏂᎬᎨᏍᏗ*, …
english: and if any of you do *lack* wisdom, …

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏔᏂ ᎿᏉ ᏥᎶᎩᎳᏗᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ, *ᎤᏅᎪᎳᏛ* ᎠᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᎶᎩᎸᎢ.
english: and it hath come to pass (in My sending a cloud over the earth) that *the bow* hath been seen in the cloud,

syllabary: … ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ *ᎤᏔᏕᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ*, ᎠᏴ ᏩᎩᎷᏥ, ᎠᎴ ᏩᏗᏔ.
english: … ‘If any one *doth thirst*, let him come unto me and drink;

syllabary: ᎡᎵᏊᏍ *ᏱᏣᏩᎯ* ᏓᏟᎶᏍᏛ? ᎥᎥ ᎠᎴ Ꮭ ᎯᏕᏯᏍᏓ.
english: Is it enough *to buy* the item? Choose between Yes and No.

syllabary: … *ᎤᏪᎷᏅᏉ* ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ, “ᎦᏙᏃ ᏓᎦᏛᏁᎵ ᏰᎵ ᎦᏴᎩᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏔᏂ?”
english: … he burst out, as he had done before, *crying*, “What shall I do to be saved?”
… because now doth *my husband* love me.'

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᎰᎵ ᏗᎧᎵᏬᎯ ᎠᏓᏍᎩᏅᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ *ᎤᏴᏍᏗ* ᎨᏒᎢ.
english: Whose mouth is full of cursing and *bitterness*.

syllabary: ᎠᎵ ᏣᎸᎯ *ᏣᎧᏛᎢ* ᎦᏚ ᎯᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎬᏂ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏫᎯᎷᏥᏌᏅᎭ, ᎾᎿᏰᏃ ᏪᏣᏯᏅᏛ; ᎦᏓᏰᏃ ᏂᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏮᏛᎯᎷᏥᏌᏂ.
english: by the sweat of *thy face* thou dost eat bread till thy return unto the ground, for out of it hast thou been taken, for dust thou [art], and unto dust thou turnest back.'

syllabary: ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᏩᏛᎲ ᎠᎩᎾ ᏐᏈᎵ-ᏗᎦᎵᎠᏅᎯᏛ, *ᎤᎩᎸᏅᎩ*, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ;
english: and Jesus having found a young ass *did sit upon* it, according as it is written,

syllabary: … ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏉ ᎠᎭ *ᎤᎦᏔ* ᎢᏥᎩᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏥᏫᏏ ᎦᏙᎯ.
english: … lo, *seed* for you, and ye have sown the ground,

syllabary: Ꮓ, *ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎵᏗ*, ᏅᎩᏁ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎠᎨᏯ ᎤᏂᏒᏛᏁᎢ ᏴᎩ ᎤᏩᏑᎦᎵ ᏗᎦᎶᏇ ᎬᏗ ᏓᏥᏍᏕᏲᎯᎮᎢ.
phonetic: Hna, *uhlsgwahldi*, nv²kị²ne⁴Ɂi gesvɁi agehya unisvdvneɁi Yvgi Uwasugali dikalogwe gvhdi datsisdeyohị³heɁi.
english: Then, *finally*, with the fourth woman they trapped Spear Finger.

syllabary: ᏧᏁᎳ ᎢᏳᏕᏗᏴᏓ ᎨᏎᎢ ᏌᎻ ᎦᏓᎫᎫ *ᎤᎵᏏ* ᎤᏩᏛᎲᎢ.
english: It was 8 o'clock when Sam Crock's *grandmother* found him.

syllabary: ᎢᎸᎯᏢᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ *ᎤᎵᏬᏨᎯ* ᏥᎦᏃᎢ, ᎾᎿ ᎠᏬᎭᎵ ᎠᎾᏓᏟᏏᏍᎪᎢ.
english: for wherever *the carcase* may be, there shall the eagles be gathered together.

syllabary: ᎠᎴ *ᎤᎶᎩᎸ* ᎤᏅᎪᎳᏖᏍᏗ; ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎦᏂᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏆᏅᏗᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏚᎾᏠᎯᏍᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏗᏅᏃᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏇᏓᎵ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏣᏁᎭ.
english: and the bow hath been in *the cloud*, and I have seen it -- to remember the covenant age-during between God and every living creature among all flesh which [is] on the earth.'

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎩᏨᏛ, ᎠᏛᏅᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ *ᎢᎦ ᎤᎶᏐᏅᎯ*, ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᏕᎬᏩᎳᏫᏤ ᏆᎴᏗ,
english: And on *the morrow* that is after the preparation, were gathered together the chief priests, and the Pharisees, unto Pilate,
A dog's long (nose) also helps him to find *food*.

syllabary: ᎴᏥᎵᏃ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᏤᎦᏈ ᏂᎦᎷᎸᏁᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏩᏳᏤᎴ *ᎤᎸᎢ*; ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏤᎦᏈ, ᏗᏂᏲᎵ ᏗᏍᎩᎧᏏ, ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᏱᏝ, ᏓᏥᏲᎤᏏ.
english: And Rachel seeth that she hath not borne to Jacob, and Rachel is envious of *her sister*, and saith unto Jacob, 'Give me sons, and if there is none -- I die.'

syllabary: ᎿᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎪᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᏥᎩ, ᎩᎶ ᎠᏕᎸ-ᏗᎦᎶᏗ *ᎤᎾᎡᏍᏗ*, ᎦᏁᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏕᎦᎶᏗ; ᎩᎶᏃ ᎠᏰᎳᏍᏗ ᎦᏅᎯᏛ ᏄᏩᏴᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏫᎦᎾᏚᎦ ᎤᏄᏬ ᎠᎴ ᏭᏩᎯ.
english: Then said he to them, ‘But, now, *he who is having* a bag, let him take [it] up, and in like manner also a scrip; and he who is not having, let him sell his garment, and buy a sword,

syllabary: … ᏦᏩ ᎦᎵᏆᏚ ᎢᏳᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎨᏎ ᏧᏂᎵ *ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎭ* ᏓᏅᎿᏗᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎠᏫ; …
english: … Joseph, a son of seventeen years, hath been enjoying *himself with his* brethren among the flock, …
Then Christian took his leave of *his Friend*.
Then said Christian, I rejoice and *tremble*.
*sell* your goods, and give alms, …

syllabary: … ᏝᏰᏃ ᏰᎵ ᏌᏉ ᎩᏢ *ᎤᏁᎬ* ᎠᎴ ᎬᎿᎨ ᏱᏅᎦᎲᎦ.
english: … because thou art not able one hair to make *white* or black;
… every one walking in the sight of *God*,

syllabary: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏤᎲ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ *ᎤᏂᎬᎨᏍᏗ*, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏔᏲᏎᎮᏍᏗ …
english: and if any of you do *lack* wisdom, let him ask from God …

syllabary: ᎠᎴ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᏅᏛ *ᎤᏂᏣᏛ* ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᎾᏨᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎿᏉ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏳᏁᏙᎴᎢ.
english: From this [time] *many* of his disciples went away backward, and were no more walking with him,

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ *ᎤᏂᏣᏘ* ᏫᎾᎾᎵᏍᏓ ᎾᎿ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎨᏒ ᎡᎶᎯ.
english: … and they increase into *a multitude* in the midst of the land.'
Count *the sheep* walking about.
and I besought thy disciples that they might cast it out, and *they were not able*.’
Color the *ice cream* a color according to the following.
At *the cave* she is looking for him. She isn't finding him.
Did not I direct thee the way to the *little* Wicket-gate?

syllabary: *ᎤᏍᏗᎢ* ᎠᎩᏙ ᎡᎭ. ᏥᎦᏴᎵᏯ ᏏᏅ ᎠᎩᏙ ᎨᏒᎢ.
english: I have a *baby* brother. I am older than my brother.
… “You should be learning doctoring medicine; I am going to teach you *where to look*”.

syllabary: ᎢᏣᏛᎦᏅᎯ ᎢᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᎦᏪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎯᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᎥ ᎢᏗᏍᏓᏓᎳ, ᎯᏍᎦᎨᏍᏗᏃ *ᏣᏍᎦᎩ*.
english: ‘Ye heard that it was said: Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and shalt hate *thine enemy*;

syllabary: … ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏥᏙᏓ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎤᎾᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎩᏁᏤᎸᎩ *ᎤᏒᎯ ᏒᏃᏱ*, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; …
english: … but the God of your father *yesternight* hath spoken unto me, saying, …
Second they are cutting *the wheat*.
and *greatly* distressed in his mind;
“Quail? Quail! Where is *my* whistle?”

syllabary: ᎠᏓᏫᏃ ᎢᏫ ᏚᏬᎡ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ, ᏂᎦᏛᏰᏃ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏅᏃᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎤᏂᏥ* ᎨᏎᎢ.
english: And the man calleth his wife's name Eve: for she hath been *mother* of all living.

syllabary: … ᎢᎬᏱᏱᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᎳᎪ ᎢᎦᎢ *ᏣᏚᎦ* ᎠᎩᏅᏏᏙᎯ.
english: … he said to the first, How much *dost thou owe* to my lord?

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ *ᏕᏣᎧᎭ* ᎦᎾᏝᎢ ᏦᎩᎾᏢᏅᎯ; ᎥᏝ ᎿᏉ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏲᎦᏘᏰᎭ ᎯᎦᏔᎲ ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏦᏥᏰᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏓ ᏙᎩᎲ ᎤᏩᏒ.
english: … and *possession of* the cattle [is] unto my lord, there hath not been left before my lord save our bodies, and our ground;
What person wears a *wool* jacket?
But how is it that *you* came *alone*?
There in his walking, was an *old* cold cave.
“I want to work and rest at *home*.”
*Daughter* of the sun.
“The little girl is very *pretty*, isn't she?”

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ *ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏅᎩ* ᏥᏌ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏅᏒᎩ.
english: … And the man *believed* the word that Jesus said to him, and was going on,

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎤᏬᎳ* ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏗᏑᏫᏍᎩ, …
english: this one *doth lodge* with a certain Simon a tanner, …

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎠᎵᏣᏙᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎦᏗᏏ *ᎤᏬᏰᏂ* ᎤᏂᏴᎲᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ, …
english: At the sight of which, Evangelist caught him by the right *hand*, saying, …
… ‘Sleep on henceforth, and *rest*! …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏣᏃᏁᎸ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎬᏅ ᎢᎠᏩᏘᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ *ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ*; …
english: … Besides, the King of Glory hath told thee, that he that will save his life *shall lose it*: …

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏍᏓᏍᏕᏍᏗ, ᎢᏍᏗᎦᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᎴ *ᎤᏲᎢ*.
english: … ye have been as God, knowing good and *evil*.'

syllabary: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏣᏍᎦᎩ ᎠᎪᏄ *ᎤᏲᏏᏍᎨᏍᏗ*, ᎯᏰᎶᎮᏍᏗ; …
english: I will recompense again, saith the Lord;’ if, then, thine enemy *doth hunger*, feed him; …
… and his nourishment was *locusts* and honey of the field.
Write *yes* or no.

syllabary: … ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏗ *ᏩᏚᎵᏏ*, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏩᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎻᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏅᏙᎩ ᎠᎾᏓᏛᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹᏂ.
english: … and a little *honey*, spices and myrrh, nuts and almonds;

syllabary: ᏇᏂ ᎦᏓᎦᎸᎥᏍᎨᏍᏗ *ᏩᏯ* ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ; ᏑᎾᎴ ᎦᏰᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏴᏛ, …
english: Benjamin! *a wolf* teareth; In the morning he eateth prey, …
I tell you, Sir, *I am going to* yonder Wicket-gate before me; …

syllabary: ᎾᏉᏃ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏓᏳᎷᏨᎯ ᎦᏁᎳ *ᏭᏯᏅᎲᎩ*, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎪᎯ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎸᏏᎸ ᎤᏛᏛᏅᎩ.
english: So *he called for* the Master of the house, who after a little time came to Christian, and asked him what he would have?

syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᏁᎭ? ᎭᏑᏯᎩ. ᏙᏗᏍᎩᏂᎥᏏ: Ꭰ) *ᏪᏌ*, Ꭱ) ᏅᏯ, Ꭲ) ᏐᏈᎵ.
english: What is being given? Choose. I will hand you them: a) *Cats*, b) Rocks, c) Horses.
…‘A pair of turtle-doves, or two young *pigeons*.’

syllabary: ᏗᎧᎸᎬᏃ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏗᏂᎶᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ *ᏭᏕᎵᎬᎢ*, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏴᏢᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎦᎾᏮᎢ, …
english: and they shall come from east and *west*, and from north and south, …
… *sufficient* for the day [is] the evil of it.
Where is *bear* cub?
*Spear* Finger killed three of them.

syllabary: ᎡᏘᏴᏃ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ ᎡᎮᎢ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ *ᏴᎩ ᎤᏩᏑᎦᎵ* ᏚᏙᎡᎢ.
english: “Long ago lived an old woman name *Spear Finger*.”

syllabary: … ᏧᏁᎶᏗ ᏭᎪᎲᎩ *ᏴᏫ* ᎤᏙᎵᏍᏙᏍᏛ ᏗᏟᎶᏍᏔᏅᎯ, ᎾᎿ ᎧᏅᏑᎸ ᎦᏛᎩ; …
english: … Christian saw the Picture of a very grave *Person* hang up against the wall; …
